surface_verification.justification: Compliance detective-audit library change (group_d applies the shared apply_amendments SSOT before D1-D5; config.py re-exports it from new events_amend.py) plus an audit_config.json D4-disable and a single event_amended data correction. No user-facing web/UI/server surface and no new IO-boundary code. Covered by the full 586-pass compliance-plugin suite, 76 shared event/config tests, the artifact-path-canon lint, and an end-to-end real-repo A-G audit probe (D5 fail -> pass, D4 skip, 0 FAILs).

behaviors.behavior: group_d honors event_amended: a spec_impact=none amendment clears a previously-flagged feature/change D5 event
Control: the same event WITHOUT the amendment still fails D5 (the amendment is what flips it)
An amendment that adds affected_frs also clears D5 (the merge path, not only spec_impact=none)
event_amended entries are dropped, never mis-evaluated as a feature/change work_completed
D2 still detects a stale FR carried by an amendment (post-merge attribution on the target)
config.apply_amendments still resolves after extraction (re-export of the shared SSOT)
Framework audit_config disables {A5.6,B7,D1,D4,G2}, each with a documented reason
evt-5aca940d correction clears D5 on the real event log and empties the compliance backlog
behaviors.evidence: end-to-end run_audit.py A-G on the worktree: D5 pass, D4 skip, 0 FAILs (43 findings)
